data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0018-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Recommendation — run 0010, focus `tasks=T12` (`AJC.picrep`, FGA Picard representability)

_Reconcile Ground after the Lane F Horizon session (Stacks 01HQ + Beck–Chevalley section formula). Tree verified green (`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`, 8558 jobs); both new closures `#print axioms`-clean (`[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). Orientation only — self-scope freely._

- **Useful context — the Lane F transport layer in `QuotScheme.lean` is now fully closed.** `pullback_tildeIso` (01HQ, via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` through the new `pullbackTilde_gammaBridge` NatIso), `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (01XJ), `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (01I8), and `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv` + N1–N4 + `_isBaseChange` + `pullback_app_isoTensor` are all axiom-clean. `QuotScheme.lean` real sorries are **8**: the 5 headline reps decls (`hilbertPolynomial`/`QuotFunctor`/`QuotScheme`/`Grassmannian`/`Grassmannian.representable`, warnings at L170/208/245/272/326) and the 3 `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_*` leaves (L5203/5262/5304). The full leftAdjointUniq recipe + v4.31 gotchas are in file-memory `pullback-tilde-01hq-closed.md`; the cone map is in `I-0086`/`I-0089`.

- **Useful context — the 3 base-change leaves are flatness-gated on T2/`AJC.fbc`, not on transport.** The `IsBaseChange` data they consume now exists sorry-free; what remains is the Stacks 02KH flatness algebra, whose own engine (`CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`) still carries 3 leaf sorries (L197 `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits`, L1646/L1712 the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields — see `I-0083`, `I-0076`). Those `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange*` / `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` files sit under T2's scope. The 5 headline decls remain deep Nitsure §4/§5 constructions awaiting a Route-C coherent-$\chi$ / Riemann–Roch substrate.

- **Consistency note — blueprint `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`.** The Lane F nodes (`lem:pullback_tildeIso`, `lem:pullbackTilde_gammaBridge`, `lem:tilde_gamma_adjunction_mathlib`, N1–N4) are complete and their `\lean{}` pointers resolve; a malformed `lem:pullback_tildeIso` header (title/`\leanok` misplaced after `\begin{lemma}`) was corrected this round. Still open: `I-0087` — the earlier gap1/gap2 **union-merge cluster is sorry-free but blueprint-absent**, leaving 13 genuine dangling `\cref`s (the full verified list is the latest `I-0087` comment); a blueprint-agent pass writing one node per union-merge decl is the scope.

- **Ops — box load ~8 mid-session; warm-cache `QuotScheme` build ≈ tens of seconds, full ≈ 1 min.** Several other `claude` sessions are alive (`pgrep -af claude`); check parent PIDs before editing/building files another session may touch. The AJC leandag cache predates this round's new `\leanok` nodes — a `horizon leandag` refresh would re-register the corrected proved-status if the DAG is consulted.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.
- **Open-open pushforward–restriction commutation**: the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` 4-step pattern (`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso(eqToIso) ≪≫ pushforwardCongr ≪≫ pushforwardComp.symm`) transfers verbatim to the square `opensInterLift V₀ U ≫ V₀.ι = (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι`; the appLE-coherence proof is the same 5 lines.
- **Pointwise scheme-morphism reasoning**: derive morphism-level equalities first (cancel isos with `cancel_mono`), then apply at points with `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`; raw `ConcreteCategory.hom`-coercion mismatches from `Set.mem_image` unfold are defeq — bridge with `have h' : U.ι y = V₀.ι x := hyx` + `show`.
- **Names**: `Opens.grothendieckTopology` (root ns), `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`, `Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage`, `IsAffineOpen.inf` (needs `[IsAffineHom (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X))]` ⟸ `IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal`), `Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion`, `IsOpenImmersion.of_comp`.
- The whole 430-LOC file (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`) compiled with ~3 error-fix iterations — the iso-chain compositions (`pushforwardComp/Congr/Id .app` chains) hit NO v4.31 transparency walls.
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- Recipe: pole-divisor purity WITHOUT normality/UFD of regular local rings (Albanese `PolePurity.lean`, axiom-clean) — reuse for any "unmixedness of principal ideals" need.

Mathlib v4.31 has NO regular⟹UFD (Auslander–Buchsbaum), NO Serre normality criterion, NO Krull-domain $R=\bigcap_{\mathrm{ht}=1}R_\mathfrak p$; `RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/` is only `Defs.lean`. Do not build those. The elementary substitute chain:

- **Swap lemma** (`mem_span_singleton_of_swap_pair`): domain, $u\ne 0$, $v$ regular mod $(u)$, $ux, vx \in (b)$ ⟹ $x \in (b)$ via $b(ut-vs)=0$. Replaces the determinant trick / integral-closedness entirely.
- **Regular pair in regular local of dim ≥ 2** (`IsRegularLocalRing.exists_swap_pair_of_two_le_ringKrullDim`): $u \in \mathfrak m\setminus\mathfrak m^2$ + project lemmas `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` and `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (00NP, AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean — CONFIRMED axiom-clean); pick $v$ lifting a nonzero element of the quotient's maximal ideal.
- **Ass-prime height bound** (`Ideal.height_le_one_of_colon_span_singleton`): prime $Q=((b):y)$ with $A_Q$ regular ⟹ $\mathrm{ht}\,Q\le 1$; the colon persists to $\mathfrak m_{A_Q}$ (map ≤ colon via `Localization.AtPrime.map_eq_maximalIdeal`, colon proper ⟹ ≤ maximalIdeal).
- **Main thm over $R_\mathfrak p$** so the associated prime lands below $\mathfrak p$ (`exists_le_isAssociatedPrime_of_isNoetherianRing` + `isAssociatedPrime_iff`); transfer with the golden Mathlib trio: `IsLocalization.height_under` (heights under comap of localization), `IsLocalization.localizationLocalizationAtPrimeIsoLocalization` (double localization ≃ₐ), `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv`.
- **Scheme wrapper traps**: state membership as `algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk z) X.functionField).range` (instances `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`, `functionField_isScalarTower`, `functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen` are all ready-made for integral X); point-from-prime via `hU.fromSpec` + `hU.isLocalization_stalk'` (takes the PrimeSpectrum point directly — no primeIdealOf round-trip); `Order.coheight z = 1` via CoheightBridge `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`. The union-merge tail earlier in the file has sorry-free gap1/gap2 substrate (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`); its blueprint nodes are still absent (`I-0087`).

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Why I stopped`. In that section, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

=== FlatStrat tail ===
`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.

iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
typed `sorry`. -/
theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) :=
  flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F

end AlgebraicGeometry

##### I-0089 open
T12/QuotScheme state after run 0010 (Lane F transport layer CLOSED) — updated triage

The Lane F affine-section-formula chain in `QuotScheme.lean` is now **fully closed** — the run-0010 "next single-session targets" (01XJ `pushforward_isQuasicoherent`, N1 baseMap naturality, 01HQ `pullback_tildeIso`) are all done, axiom-clean.

- **QuotScheme sorries now 8** (was 11 → 10 → 8): the **5 headline** reps decls (`hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`, `QuotScheme`, `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` — all deep, Mathlib-v4.31-gapped) + **3 `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_*`** leaves. The 3 base-change leaves are **flatness-gated on T2/`AJC.fbc`** (Stacks 02KH) — the transport layer was their last non-flatness prerequisite, so the needed `IsBaseChange` data now exists sorry-free.
- **Closed this arc (all axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`)**: `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (01I8, via union-merge substrate), `pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (01XJ, MV-induction + `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`), `pullback_tildeIso` (01HQ, **uniqueness of left adjoints** through the new `pullbackTilde_gammaBridge` NatIso — see file-memory `pullback-tilde-01hq-closed.md`), and `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv` + N1–N4 + `_isBaseChange` + `pullback_app_isoTensor` (Beck–Chevalley section formula).
- **File layout**: the Lane F chain lives at the END of `QuotScheme.lean` in a fresh `namespace AlgebraicGeometry` (private decls; `\lean{}` pointers use `AlgebraicGeometry.<name>`). The union-merge tail earlier in the file has sorry-free gap1/gap2 substrate (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`); its blueprint nodes are still absent (`I-0087`).
- **Remaining real progress on `AJC.picrep` needs**: (1) T2/`AJC.fbc` flatness algebra (unblocks the 3 base-change leaves); (2) a Route-C coherent-$\chi$ / Riemann–Roch substrate (the 5 headline decls). Neither is single-session. See triage `I-0086`.

##### I-0087 open
Picard_QuotScheme.tex: the gap1/gap2 union-merge machinery has NO blueprint nodes and many dangling crefs

The GR-quot union-merge Lean machinery in `QuotScheme.lean` (gap1 `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`, gap2 `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`, Piece A `isQuasicoherent_pullback_of_isOpenImmersion`/`_fromSpec`, the Hfr/descent chain) is **sorry-free but blueprint-absent**.

- The chapter's prose (subsections "Section-transport producer", "G1-assemble") `\cref`s labels that exist nowhere: `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, `lem:section_localization_descent`, `lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`, `lem:section_localization_hfr_basicOpen`, `lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport`, and more.

**`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.

- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.

text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.
The canonical mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` still exists in `FlatBaseChange.lean`. Let me read it plus the nerve infrastructure.

30	```
31	  X' --g'--> X
32	  |f'        |f
33	  v          v
34	  S' --g---> S
35	```
36	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
37	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
38	
39	The main declarations are:
40	
41	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
42	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
43	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction (kept for reference; the live route never forms
44	  this mate).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` — the concrete-tilde
46	  affine base-change isomorphism for a ring pushout square, assembled from the
47	  tilde dictionaries and `cancelBaseChange`; this is the brick consumed by the
48	  Čech route to Stacks 02KH (`cech_flatBaseChange` in
49	  `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`).
50	
51	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
52	
53	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
54	Tag 02KH.
55	-/
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	open Scheme.Modules
64	
65	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
66	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
67	
68	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
69	
70	Given a commutative square
71	```
72	  X' --g'--> X
73	  |f'        |f
74	  v          v
75	  S' --g---> S
76	```
77	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
78	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
79	
80	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
81	```
82	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
83	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
84	```
85	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
86	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
87	commutativity of the square.
88	
89	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
90	base-change diagram. -/
91	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
92	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
93	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
94	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
95	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
96	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
97	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
98	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
99	
100	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — locality of isomorphisms for `Scheme.Modules`
101	
102	Mathlib provides the per-open criterion `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
103	(`IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U)`) and the stalkwise criterion
104	`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` for `TopCat.Sheaf`-valued morphisms,
105	but it does not package the stalk-local criterion at the level of `Scheme.Modules`
106	morphisms. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they are the locality tools needed for
107	the affine reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (where one checks the
108	base-change map after restricting to / taking stalks over affine opens). -/
109	
110	/-- **Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism `φ : M ⟶ N`
111	of sheaves of modules over a scheme `X` is an isomorphism iff its underlying morphism of
112	abelian presheaves is a stalkwise isomorphism. Project-local: Mathlib only ships the
113	per-open criterion and the `TopCat.Sheaf`-level stalk criterion separately. -/
114	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
115	    (φ : M ⟶ N) :
116	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
117	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
118	  constructor
119	  · intro h x
120	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := Functor.map_isIso _ _
121	    exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
122	  · intro h
123	    -- Package the underlying abelian presheaves as `TopCat.Sheaf`es.
124	    let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
125	    let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
126	    let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
127	    haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := h
128	    haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
129	    have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) :=
130	      (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
131	    exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
132	
133	/-- **Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** If `B` is a basis of
134	opens of `X` and `φ : M ⟶ N` restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every basic
135	open `B i`, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This reduces iso-checking from *all* opens (the
136	content of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`) to a chosen basis. Project-local:
137	Mathlib provides the stalkwise pieces (`germ_exist_of_isBasis`,
138	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`) but not the packaged criterion at the
139	`Scheme.Modules` level. -/
140	theorem Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
141	    {ι : Type*} {B : ι → X.Opens} (hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range B))
142	    (φ : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ i, IsIso (φ.app (B i))) : IsIso φ := by
143	  -- Reduce to a stalkwise isomorphism of the underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism `α`.
144	  rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map]
145	  intro x
146	  -- `α.app (op (B i))` is definitionally `φ.app (B i)`, hence an isomorphism on each basic open.
147	  have happ : ∀ U ∈ Set.range B,
148	      IsIso (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U)) := by
149	    rintro U ⟨i, rfl⟩; exact h i

140	
141	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
142	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
143	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
144	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
145	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
146	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
147	
148	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
149	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
150	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
151	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
152	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
153	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
154	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
155	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
156	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
157	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
158	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
159	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
160	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
161	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
162	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
163	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
164	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
165	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
166	      (Over.w g)) ≫
167	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
168	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
169	      (Over.w g))
170	
171	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
172	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
173	X.Modules` requires
174	```
175	  pushPullMap_id   : pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y)
176	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g
177	```
178	Both laws are proved axiom-clean below (see `pushPullMap_id` and `pushPullMap_comp`),
179	and `pushPullFunctor` is assembled from them immediately after. -/
180	
181	/-! ### Functor laws of the push–pull functor `G` -/
182	
183	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
184	/-- Identity law of the push–pull functor `G`. -/
185	lemma pushPullMap_id (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) :
186	    pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y) := by
187	  -- `star`: the unit-triangle for the identity adjunction `pullback 𝟙 ⊣ pushforward 𝟙`.
188	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := Y.left.Modules))
189	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left))
190	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
191	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
192	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
193	  -- `hru`: right-unitality of the pullback pseudofunctor, applied at `F`.

2293	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
2294	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
2295	
2296	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
2297	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
2298	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
2299	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
2300	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.
2301	
2302	\begin{definition}
2303	
2304	\leanok
2305	[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
2306	  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
2307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
2308	  % NOTE: The definition carries no \uses: the Lean construction is
2309	  % `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals (fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X,U) Γ(F,U))`, which is well-defined
2310	  % unconditionally and does NOT depend on the localization bridges at definition time. The two
2311	  % facts lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map and lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen are needed
2312	  % only for the downstream CHARACTERIZATION (the reverse inclusion / equality on affine opens, the
2313	  % "annihilator forward direction"), which is not yet a blueprint block.
2314	  % NOTE: The definition itself is formalized and axiom-clean; the inclusion direction
2315	  % \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U) \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\)
2316	  % (\cref{lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le}) is closed. The reverse inclusion, which identifies
2317	  % the annihilator sheaf with the module-annihilator on affine opens, remains bridge-gated on
2318	  % \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
2319	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
2320	  % Polynomials" (read from
2321	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2322	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2323	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2324	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2325	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2326	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2327	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2328	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
2329	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
2330	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
2331	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
2332	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
2333	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
2334	  \(U \subseteq X\),
2335	  \[
2336	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
2337	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
2338	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
2339	  \]
2340	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
2341	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
2342	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
2343	
2344	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
2345	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
2346	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
2347	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
2348	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
2349	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
2350	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
2351	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
2352	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
2353	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
2354	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
2355	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2356	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
2357	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
2358	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
2359	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
2360	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
2361	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
2362	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
2363	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
2364	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
2365	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
2366	
2367	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not carry an annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
2368	\end{definition}

2543	  The functor of \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib})
2544	  is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the \(U\)-slice \(J_X|_U\) of the opens Grothendieck

2547	\begin{proof}
2548	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}
2549	  A sieve covers in the slice topology \(J_X|_U\) iff its image sieve covers in \(J_X\), and
2550	  covering for the opens topology is pointwise: a sieve covers iff its members' opens cover the
2551	  base set-theoretically. Given a point \(x\) of a slice object and a cover member in \(J_U\)
2552	  containing the image of \(x\), transport it across the open embedding \(U \hookrightarrow X\),
2553	  which is injective: the set-theoretic image of an open \(V \subseteq U\) is an open of \(X\)
2554	  contained in the base, a neighbourhood of \(x\) lying in the image sieve. This witnesses the
2555	  cover-lifting condition.

2641	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2642	[The Grothendieck-slice restriction equals the geometric restriction (gap1, C)]
2643	  \label{lem:over_restrict_iso}
2644	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictIso}
2645	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib,
2646	    lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib, def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr,
2647	    lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous, lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous,
2648	    lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}
2649	  % NOTE: RESOLVED and axiom-clean. AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictIso
2650	  % now exists (#print axioms = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}); gap1 bridge C
2651	  % (steps 1->4) is fully closed. The step-2 geometric ring-sheaf identification collapsed to
2652	  % `rfl` (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf is definitionally the transport of the sliced sheaf, via
2653	  % toScheme_presheaf_obj/map). The statement is routed through the step-3 equivalence functor:
2654	  % overRestrictIso : (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M.
2655	  \textit{The one lemma that must touch the Grothendieck slice site; everything downstream of it is
2656	  geometric.}
2657	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open. The \emph{abstract Grothendieck-slice}
2658	  restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) — an object of the category of sheaves of modules over the
2659	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) on the slice site \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U\) —
2660	  is canonically isomorphic to the \emph{geometric} restriction
2661	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\)
2662	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}), equivalently to the pullback \(U.\iota^{*} M\)
2663	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), as a sheaf of modules on the small Zariski
2664	  site of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\). The isomorphism is induced by the equivalence
2665	  of sites \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U \simeq \mathrm{Opens}(U.\mathrm{toScheme})\) coming from the opens
2666	  functor of the open immersion \(U.\iota\), under which the sliced sheaf of rings
2667	  \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) corresponds to the structure sheaf of \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\).
2668	  For \(X = \Spec R\) and \(U = D(r)\) a basic open, the target category is
2669	  \((\Spec R_r).\mathrm{Modules}\) under the affine identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\)
2670	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
2671	\end{lemma}
2672	\begin{proof}
2673	  \uses{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr, lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous,
2674	    lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous, lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib,
2675	    lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib,
2676	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2677	  The isomorphism is built in four steps, ascending from the purely topological layer to the
2678	  module-level statement.
2679	
2680	  \emph{Step 1 (topological layer --- done).} The opens functor of the open immersion
2681	  \(U.\iota\) realizes the slice of the opens site of \(X\) over \(U\) as the opens site of the
2682	  subspace \(U\); on sheaf categories this is the equivalence
2683	  \(\mathrm{Sheaf}(J_X|_U,\,A) \simeq \mathrm{Sheaf}(J_U,\,A)\) of
2684	  \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}, for every target category \(A\). This is the
2685	  topos-theoretic layer, assembled from the cocontinuity/continuity of both legs of
2686	  \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}).
2687	
2688	  \emph{Step 2 (geometric ring-sheaf identification --- the current obstacle).} Identify the
2689	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) with the structure sheaf
2690	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U}\) of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\), transported across the
2691	  \(\mathrm{RingCat}\)-valued instance of \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}; this is an
2692	  isomorphism of sheaves of rings. The bridge is the factorization of the opens functor of the
2693	  immersion,
2694	  \[
2695	    U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}
2696	      \;=\; (\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U).\mathrm{inverse}
2697	        \;\circ\; \mathrm{Over.forget}\,U ,
2698	  \]
2699	  under which the structure-sheaf comparison reduces to the standard open-immersion
2700	  structure-sheaf identifications; recall that \(\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota\)
2701	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}) is precisely pushforward along

2702	  \(U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}\). This ring-sheaf identification is the remaining geometric
2703	  obstacle.
2704	
2705	  \emph{Step 3 (lift to modules).} Given the step-2 identification of the underlying ring sheaves
2706	  and the continuity of both legs (\cref{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous},
2707	  \cref{lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous}), lift the sheaf-of-rings comparison to an
2708	  equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules via
2709	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}\)
2710	  (\cref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}). Under this equivalence the sliced
2711	  module \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) corresponds to the geometric restriction
2712	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\).
2713	
2714	  \emph{Step 4 (compose).} Compose with
2715	  \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib})
2716	  to land the isomorphism \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U \cong M.\mathrm{restrict}\,U.\iota \cong
2717	  U.\iota^{*}M\). The two sides live a priori in different module categories, so the literal Lean
2718	  statement of \(\mathrm{overRestrictIso}\) is phrased through the step-3 equivalence functor; for
2719	  \(X = \Spec R\) and \(U = D(r)\) the target is \((\Spec R_r).\mathrm{Modules}\) under
2720	  \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
2721	\end{proof}

2733	  \textit{The pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge; the form consumed by the P1 transport.}
2734	  With \(X\) and \(U \subseteq X\) as above and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\), the transport of the
2735	  abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) under the step-3 equivalence functor
2736	  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) is canonically isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) of \(M\)
2737	  along the open immersion \(U.\iota\):

2739	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
2740	      \;\cong\; (U.\iota^{*})\,M

2743	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}; it is exactly the form in which a presentation of

2746	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}.
2747	\end{lemma}
2748	\begin{proof}
2749	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
2750	  Compose the slice-to-geometric isomorphism \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}, which identifies the
2751	  transported slice restriction with the geometric restriction

2754	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) identifying the geometric restriction with

2832	\(R\)-level statement through the combiner \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}; the four
2833	sub-lemmas below supply the geometric inputs (an iterated-pullback \(\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) iso, the

2858	[Schematic support of a sheaf of modules]
2859	  \label{def:schematic_support}
2860	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupport}
2861	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator, lem:modules_annihilator_ideal_le,
2862	    def:schematic_support_immersion}
2863	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
2864	  % Polynomials" (read from
2865	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2866	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2867	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2868	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2869	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
2870	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
2871	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2872	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2873	  \emph{schematic support} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the closed subscheme of \(X\)
2874	  cut out by its annihilator ideal sheaf (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}):
2875	  \[
2876	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F})
2877	    \;=\; V\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\hookrightarrow\; X,
2878	  \]
2879	  equipped with its canonical closed immersion into \(X\). This realises
2880	  Nitsure's ``schematic support of \(\mathcal{F}\)'' as a closed subscheme of
2881	  \(X\).
2882	\end{definition}

2884	\begin{definition}\leanok
2885	[Schematic-support immersion]
2886	  \label{def:schematic_support_immersion}
2887	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupportι}
2888	  \uses{def:schematic_support, def:modules_annihilator}
2889	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2890	  \emph{schematic-support immersion} is the canonical closed immersion
2891	  \[
2892	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \;\hookrightarrow\; X
2893	  \]
2894	  of the schematic support (\cref{def:schematic_support}) into the ambient
2895	  scheme, namely the subscheme immersion of the annihilator ideal sheaf
2896	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}). It is a
2897	  quasi-compact preimmersion onto the support, and it is the morphism whose
2898	  composite with a base morphism \(f : X \to S\) defines proper support
2899	  (\cref{def:has_proper_support}). Proved directly in Lean.
2900	\end{definition}

2902	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
2903	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
2904	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
2905	  \mathlibok
2906	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2907	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
2908	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
2909	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
2910	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
2911	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
2912	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
2913	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
2914	\end{lemma}
2915	
2916	\begin{definition}
2917	
2918	\leanok
2919	[Proper support over the base]
2920	  \label{def:has_proper_support}
2921	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport}
2922	  \uses{def:schematic_support, def:schematic_support_immersion, lem:isProper_mathlib}
2923	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors $\Hilb_{X/S}$ and
2924	  % $\Quot_{E/X/S}$" (read from
2925	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L407-L409
2926	  % + L418-L419).
2927	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2928	  % "(i) a coherent sheaf $\F$ on $X_T = X\times_ST$
2929	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
2930	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with
2931	  %  [...]
2932	  %  Then as properness and flatness are preserved
2933	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,"
2934	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2935	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
2936	  \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism. We say \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has proper support
2937	  over \(S\) (along \(f\))} when the composite morphism
2938	  \[
2939	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \hookrightarrow X \xrightarrow{f} S
2940	  \]
2941	  is proper (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}). Since properness is stable under base
2942	  change (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}), this condition is preserved by pull-back
2943	  along any \(S'\to S\), which is exactly what the well-definedness of the Quot
2944	  functor's pullback action requires.
2945	\end{definition}

2947	\begin{definition}
2948	
2949	\leanok
2950	[Locally free of rank \(d\)]
2951	  \label{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
2952	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}
2953	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises (2)" and
2954	  % "Grassmannian of a vector bundle" (read from
2955	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L562-L564
2956	  % + L584-L585).
2957	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2958	  % "$\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2959	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2960	  %  [...]
2961	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
2962	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,"
2963	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2964	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
2965	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). We say \(\mathcal{M}\) is \emph{locally free of rank
2966	  \(d\)} when there exists an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) together with
2967	  isomorphisms \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus d}\) for
2968	  each \(i\).
2969	
2970	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not provide this predicate.
2971	\end{definition}
2972	
2973	\section{The Quot functor}
2974	\label{sec:quot_functor}
2975

643	    X' X _ _ g' (pullback.fst (𝒰.f i ≫ f) g)

651	/-- **The base-changed cover intersection is the preimage of the intersection** (Stacks 02KG;
652	carved block `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq`).  For the base-changed cover
653	`𝒰' = (openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom` of `X' = X ×_S S'` and a
654	*finite* index family `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`, the Čech intersection open of `𝒰'` is the `g'`-preimage of
655	the intersection open of `𝒰`:
656	```
657	  coverInterOpen 𝒰' σ = (g')⁻¹(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).
658	```
659	Per member `coverOpen_baseChange_eq` gives the preimage identity, and preimage commutes with the
660	finite intersection (`coe_iInf_of_finite` + `Set.preimage_iInter`).  Finiteness of `κ` is genuinely
661	needed (the `Opens.map` frame hom preserves only *finite* meets); the Čech use is over
662	`Fin (n+1)`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq`. -/
663	theorem coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
664	    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} [Finite κ] (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
665	    coverInterOpen ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom) σ
666	      = g' ⁻¹ᵁ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by
667	  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
668	  rw [coverInterOpen, coverInterOpen, coe_iInf_of_finite, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe,
669	    coe_iInf_of_finite, Set.preimage_iInter]
670	  refine Set.iInter_congr fun k => ?_
671	  have hk := coverOpen_baseChange_eq f g f' g' h 𝒰 (σ k)
672	  simp only [coverOpen]
673	  rw [hk, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]
674	
675	/-- **Bare Beck–Chevalley mate** for the restricted cartesian square `IsPullback gV p' p g'`
676	(`gV ≫ p = p' ≫ g'`).  This is the canonical base-change natural transformation
677	`g'^* ∘ p_* ⟶ p'_* ∘ gV^*` obtained as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform) — under the
678	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions for `p` and `p'` — of the canonical pullback 2-isomorphism
679	`pullback g' ⋙ pullback p' ≅ pullback p ⋙ pullback gV` coming from `p' ≫ g' = gV ≫ p`.
680	
681	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness, no open-immersion hypothesis): it is the
682	*comparison map* whose being an iso is the genuine Beck–Chevalley content.  It is a 6-line local
683	restatement of the (sorry-tainted-`QuotScheme`) `canonicalBaseChangeMap` so that this file does
684	not import `QuotScheme`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_beckchevalley` (mate). -/
685	noncomputable def openImmersion_bareBC {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
686	    (g' : X' ⟶ X) (p : V ⟶ X) (p' : V' ⟶ X') (gV : V' ⟶ V)
687	    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') :
688	    pushforward p ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⟶
689	      Scheme.Modules.pullback gV ⋙ pushforward p' :=
690	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
691	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p)
692	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p')
693	    (((pullbackComp p' g') ≪≫
694	      pullbackCongr hsq.w.symm ≪≫
695	      (pullbackComp gV p).symm).hom)
696	
697	/-- **Pullback telescope across the restricted cartesian square** (the pseudofunctor leg of the
698	open-immersion Beck–Chevalley).  Using only the pseudofunctor structure of `pullback`
699	(`pullbackComp`, `pullbackCongr`) and the square equation `p' ≫ g' = gV ≫ p`, the iterated
700	pullback `p'^*(g'^* F)` is canonically isomorphic to `gV^*(p^* F)`:
701	```
702	  p'^*(g'^* F) ≅ (p' ≫ g')^* F = (gV ≫ p)^* F ≅ gV^*(p^* F).
703	```
704	Sorry-free, build-cheap (no flatness, no affineness).  Together with `openImmersion_bareBC` this
705	collapses `openImmersion_beckChevalley` to the single obligation `IsIso (openImmersion_bareBC …)`.
706	Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_beckchevalley` (telescope). -/
707	noncomputable def openImmersion_bc_telescope {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
708	    (g' : X' ⟶ X) (p : V ⟶ X) (p' : V' ⟶ X') (gV : V' ⟶ V)
709	    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') (F : X.Modules) :
710	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) ≅
711	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gV).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj F) :=
712	  ((pullbackComp p' g') ≪≫
713	      pullbackCongr hsq.w.symm ≪≫
714	      (pullbackComp gV p).symm).app F
715	
716	/-- **The base-changed edge `p'` of the restricted cartesian square is an open immersion.**
717	For the cartesian square `hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g'` (so `p' : V' ⟶ X'` is the base change of
718	`p` along `g'`), if `p` is an open immersion then so is `p'` — open immersions are stable under
719	base change (`MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange @IsOpenImmersion`).  This is the
720	open-immersion-ness of the *left* edge of the square that the sectionwise cover-refinement route
721	of `openImmersion_beckChevalley` (Stage 2) consumes: it is what lets `pushforward p'` / `pullback p'`
722	be identified with restriction along `p'` (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback p'`) on the target side of
723	the bare Beck–Chevalley mate.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_beckchevalley` (left-edge
724	open-immersion side-condition). -/
725	theorem isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
726	    (g' : X' ⟶ X) (p : V ⟶ X) (p' : V' ⟶ X') (gV : V' ⟶ V)
727	    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') [IsOpenImmersion p] : IsOpenImmersion p' :=
728	  MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback hsq ‹IsOpenImmersion p›
729	
730	/-! ## Stage-2 reduction: the bare mate factors as `unit ≫ iso`
731	
732	The mate formula (`mateEquiv_apply`) exhibits `openImmersion_bareBC` at each object `c` as
733	```
734	  unit_{p'} (g'^*(p_* c)) ≫ p'_*(telescope-iso) ≫ p'_*(gV^*(counit_p c)).
735	```
736	For an *open immersion* `p`, the counit of the geometric adjunction
737	`pullback p ⊣ pushforward p` is an isomorphism — it is conjugate, under the
738	`leftAdjointUniq` comparison `restrictFunctorIsoPullback p`, to the counit of the
739	site-level `restrictAdjunction p`, invertible in Mathlib.  Hence the *only* non-iso
740	factor is the leading `p'`-unit, and `IsIso (bareBC.app c)` collapses to the single
741	node `IsIso (unit_{p'}.app (g'^*(p_* c)))` — "`g'^*(p_* c)` is in the essential image
742	of `p'_*`".  This is a strict sharpening of the blueprint Stage-2 chain
743	(`lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso`): the legs-are-restrictions reduction
744	(`lem:openimm_bareBC_legs_restriction`) is here replaced by the exact mate
745	factorization, so the member/assembly work only ever has to handle the unit. -/
746	
747	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
748	/-- **The geometric counit at an open immersion is an isomorphism.**  For an open
749	immersion `q`, the counit `q^*(q_* c) ⟶ c` of the geometric adjunction
750	`pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` is invertible: by
751	`Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit` it factors the counit of the site-level
752	`restrictAdjunction q` — an isomorphism in Mathlib (`restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso`) —
753	through the `leftAdjointUniq` comparison (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback q`, an iso).
754	Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso`. -/
755	theorem openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso {V : Scheme.{u}} (q : V ⟶ X)
756	    [IsOpenImmersion q] (c : V.Modules) :
757	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q).counit.app c) := by
758	  haveI hnat : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction q).counit) :=
759	    inferInstanceAs (IsIso (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)
760	  haveI happ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction q).counit.app c) :=
761	    NatIso.isIso_app_of_isIso _ c
762	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit
763	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction q) (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)
764	
765	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
766	/-- **Mate factorization of the bare Beck–Chevalley comparison.**  At each `c : V.Modules`
767	the mate `openImmersion_bareBC` is, per the `mateEquiv` formula, the `p'`-unit at
768	`g'^*(p_* c)` followed by `p'_*` of the three pullback-telescope iso components and
769	`p'_*(gV^*(-))` of the `p`-counit.  Every factor after the unit is an isomorphism.
770	Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso` (factorization). -/
771	theorem openImmersion_bareBC_app_eq {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
772	    (g' : X' ⟶ X) (p : V ⟶ X) (p' : V' ⟶ X') (gV : V' ⟶ V)
773	    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') (c : V.Modules) :
774	    (openImmersion_bareBC g' p p' gV hsq).app c =
775	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p').unit.app
776	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj c)) ≫
777	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p').map
778	          ((pullbackComp p' g').hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj c)) ≫
779	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p').map
780	          ((pullbackCongr hsq.w.symm).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj c)) ≫
781	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p').map
782	          ((pullbackComp gV p).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj c)) ≫
783	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p').map ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gV).map
784	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).counit.app c)) := by
785	  simp [openImmersion_bareBC, mateEquiv_apply]
786	  erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]
787	  rfl
788	
789	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
790	/-- **`IsIso bareBC` collapses to the unit node.**  Since the telescope components and the
791	`p`-counit factor (`openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso`) are isomorphisms, the mate
792	`openImmersion_bareBC` is an isomorphism at `c` as soon as the `p'`-unit is one at
793	`g'^*(p_* c)` — i.e. as soon as `g'^*(p_* c)` lies in the essential image of `p'_*`.
794	Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso` (reduction). -/
795	theorem openImmersion_bareBC_app_isIso_of_unit {V V' : Scheme.{u}}
796	    (g' : X' ⟶ X) (p : V ⟶ X) (p' : V' ⟶ X') (gV : V' ⟶ V)
797	    (hsq : IsPullback gV p' p g') [IsOpenImmersion p] (c : V.Modules)
798	    (hu : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p').unit.app
799	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj c)))) :
800	    IsIso ((openImmersion_bareBC g' p p' gV hsq).app c) := by
801	  rw [openImmersion_bareBC_app_eq]
802	  haveI := hu
803	  haveI := openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso p c
804	  infer_instance
805	
806	/-- **Unit-iso from essential-image membership** (open-immersion case).  For an open
807	immersion `p'`, the pushforward `p'_*` is fully faithful (Mathlib instances on
808	`restrictAdjunction`), so by `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` the unit of
809	`pullback p' ⊣ pushforward p'` is an isomorphism at every module in the essential image
810	of `p'_*`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso` (essential-image form). -/
811	theorem openImmersion_unit_isIso_of_essImage {V' : Scheme.{u}} (p' : V' ⟶ X')
812	    [IsOpenImmersion p'] (M : X'.Modules)
813	    (h : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p').essImage M) :
814	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p').unit.app M) := by
815	  obtain ⟨K, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
816	  exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p').isIso_unit_app_of_iso e.symm
817	
818	/-! ## Stage-2 assembly: essential-image membership is cover-local
819	
820	The essential-image node is verified member-by-member on an open cover of `X'`.  The
821	engine is elementary: membership `M ∈ essImage p'_*` is equivalent (fully-faithful
822	`p'_*`) to invertibility of the `p'`-unit at `M`; transported to the site-level
823	`restrictAdjunction p'`, the unit's components are the plain presheaf restrictions
824	`M(U) → M(U ∩ ran p')`, so cover-conservativity (`isIso_iff_isIso_restrict`) applies
825	with NO pushforward/restriction commutation coherence: the restricted morphism again
826	has plain `M.presheaf.map` components, compared against the member datum through a
827	lattice identity of opens. -/
828	
829	/-- Component of a restricted morphism of modules is the component at the image open.
830	Definitional. Project-local; blueprint `lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local` (component). -/
831	theorem restrictFunctor_map_app {W : Scheme.{u}} (w : W ⟶ X') [IsOpenImmersion w]
832	    {M N : X'.Modules} (ψ : M ⟶ N) (O : W.Opens) :
833	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor w).map ψ).app O = ψ.app (w ''ᵁ O) := rfl
834	
835	/-- **Restriction maps of a pushforward-presented module are isomorphisms.**  If
836	`N : W.Modules` is isomorphic to a pushforward `(O₀.ι)_* K` from the open `O₀ ⊆ W`, then
837	for every open `O ⊆ W` the presheaf restriction `N(O) → N(O ⊓ O₀)` is an isomorphism: on
838	the pushforward side it is `K.presheaf.map` of the identity inclusion
839	`O₀.ι⁻¹(O ⊓ O₀) = O₀.ι⁻¹(O)` (an `eqToHom`), and the comparison iso conjugates.

799	end AlgebraicGeometry
